prompt: A warehouse operative reports lower back pain after repeated lifting of 20 kg sacks from floor level. Which initial control is most aligned with the hierarchy of control?

options.text: Provide a mechanical lift or raise the load source to waist height to reduce bending and manual exertion
Issue a back support belt and continue floor-level lifting
Rotate workers faster without changing the task
Tell the worker to lift with a straight back only

explanation: Engineering and task redesign that reduce bending and load handling at floor level address the musculoskeletal hazard at source.

distractorExplanations.b: PPE such as belts does not eliminate manual handling risk and is low in the hierarchy.
distractorExplanations.c: Rotation shares exposure but does not remove the hazardous task design.
distractorExplanations.d: Technique advice alone is insufficient where task design drives risk.

prompt: Under typical manual handling assessment principles, which factor combination most increases risk of injury?

options.text: Light load, waist-height pick-up, team lift, and frequent rest breaks
Heavy load, awkward posture, limited grip, and insufficient recovery time between lifts
Automated conveyor delivery with no lifting required
Load close to the body with handles and clear footing

explanation: TILE (task, individual, load, environment) risk rises with weight, posture, grip difficulty, and fatigue from inadequate recovery.

distractorExplanations.a: Favourable factors reduce manual handling risk.
distractorExplanations.c: Automation removes manual handling where applied.
distractorExplanations.d: Good posture and load design lower risk.

prompt: An office worker using a laptop on a sofa for eight hours daily complains of neck and wrist pain. What is the best first intervention?

options.text: Recommend painkillers only without changing the workstation
Increase meeting frequency to reduce typing time only
Provide a suitable DSE workstation with adjustable chair, external keyboard/mouse, and screen at eye level
Allow sofa working because it is more comfortable

explanation: DSE regulations expect suitable equipment and workstation setup for habitual screen users — laptop-on-sofa is a common ergonomic failure.

distractorExplanations.a: Medical treatment does not replace ergonomic control of the hazard.
distractorExplanations.b: Meetings do not correct sustained poor posture at the desk.
distractorExplanations.d: Comfort perception does not equal ergonomic suitability.

prompt: Which exposure pattern is most associated with noise-induced hearing loss?

options.text: Brief exposure to low-level background music in an office
Wearing correctly fitted hearing protection during high-noise tasks
Using quieter tools after a noise risk assessment
Regular exposure above action levels without adequate hearing protection or engineering controls

explanation: NIHL develops from cumulative exposure to high noise; action values trigger controls including engineering, administrative, and PPE measures.

distractorExplanations.a: Low office noise is typically below harmful exposure for NIHL.
distractorExplanations.b: Low office noise is typically below harmful exposure for NIHL.
distractorExplanations.c: Hearing protection reduces risk when correctly used.

prompt: A worker operating a jackhammer reports tingling fingers and whitened fingertips in cold weather. The most likely hazard is:

options.text: Hand-arm vibration syndrome (HAVS) from prolonged use of vibrating tools
Exposure to office display screen equipment only
Static standing on an anti-fatigue mat
Inhalation of water mist from a humidifier

explanation: HAVS is linked to repeated exposure to hand-arm vibration from tools such as breakers and grinders, especially without control or health surveillance.

distractorExplanations.b: DSE relates to screen work, not vibration injury.
distractorExplanations.c: Standing alone does not cause vibration white finger.
distractorExplanations.d: Humidifier mist is unrelated to HAVS.

prompt: Which welfare provision is most important for workers on a remote outdoor construction site in summer?

options.text: Removal of all break times to accelerate completion
Shelter, potable drinking water, toilet facilities, and rest breaks to manage heat stress
Single unmarked water hose without hygiene facilities
No shade because workers should acclimatize instantly

explanation: Welfare facilities support physical health — hydration, sanitation, rest, and shelter are basic expectations on demanding sites.

distractorExplanations.a: Removing breaks increases heat stress and fatigue risk.
distractorExplanations.c: Inadequate water and sanitation breach welfare standards.
distractorExplanations.d: Acclimatization requires time and controlled exposure, not denial of shelter.

prompt: A call centre manager notices rising sickness absence citing stress and anxiety. The most appropriate organisational response is:

options.text: Dismiss stress as a personal problem unrelated to work
Increase monitoring of keystrokes to raise productivity
Review workload, targets, support resources, and consultation with workers to identify psychosocial risk factors
Ban all absence reports to improve statistics

explanation: Psychological health hazards include excessive demand, poor support, and bullying; employers should assess and control work-related stressors.

distractorExplanations.a: Work-related stress must be managed, not dismissed.
distractorExplanations.b: Work-related stress must be managed, not dismissed.
distractorExplanations.d: Suppressing reporting hides the problem and harms culture.

prompt: What is the primary purpose of a Display Screen Equipment (DSE) assessment?

options.text: Certify that the employee needs no breaks for five years
Replace the need for lighting in the office
Guarantee zero eyesight changes for all users
Identify ergonomic risks from screen work and implement adjustments to reduce musculoskeletal and visual strain

explanation: DSE assessments review workstation layout, breaks, lighting, and equipment to reduce RSI, eye strain, and related disorders.

distractorExplanations.a: DSE assessment does not remove general lighting duties.
distractorExplanations.b: DSE assessment does not remove general lighting duties.
distractorExplanations.c: Vision can change; assessments reduce strain but do not guarantee medical outcomes.

prompt: During a noise survey, the daily personal noise exposure reaches 90 dB(A) with peak levels below the upper action value. Mandatory actions include:

options.text: Hearing protection zone, training, and health surveillance where required, plus programme to reduce exposure
No action until workers report total deafness
Remove all machine guarding instead of addressing noise
Rely on cotton wool as an equivalent to rated ear defenders

explanation: Upper exposure action values trigger a programme of control including PPE provision, training, and surveillance alongside noise reduction.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.c: Guarding addresses different hazards, not noise substitution.
distractorExplanations.d: Cotton wool is not approved hearing protection.

prompt: Which manual handling technique reduces spinal loading most effectively when lifting is unavoidable?

options.text: Twist at the waist while holding the load at arm's length
Keep the load close to the body, use leg muscles, avoid twisting, and team-lift if the load is unstable or too heavy
Lift quickly with a jerking motion to reduce time under load
Bend the back fully while keeping legs straight

explanation: Safe lifting limits reach, twisting, and spinal flexion; team lifting shares load when assessment indicates.

distractorExplanations.a: Twisting and extended reach increase disc stress.
distractorExplanations.c: Jerking increases peak forces on the spine.
distractorExplanations.d: Straight-leg stooping loads the lower back heavily.

prompt: An employer introduces job rotation for assembly line workers doing highly repetitive wrist movements. The main limitation of rotation alone is:

options.text: It always eliminates the need for workstation redesign
It guarantees workers need no training
It may spread exposure to different MSD risks without reducing total ergonomic hazard in the job design
It removes the need for health surveillance for HAVS

explanation: Rotation can reduce monotony but may not lower overall biomechanical exposure unless tasks are redesigned with lower force and repetition.

distractorExplanations.a: Redesign and engineering controls may still be necessary.
distractorExplanations.b: Training needs remain for each task.
distractorExplanations.d: HAVS surveillance depends on vibration exposure, not rotation alone.

prompt: Whole-body vibration (WBV) is most commonly associated with which work activity?

options.text: Typing at a standard office keyboard
Reading paper documents under desk lamps
Walking on level carpeted corridors
Driving off-road plant such as dumpers and forklifts on uneven ground for extended periods

explanation: WBV affects drivers of vehicles transmitting vibration through the seat; risk assessments and seat suspension limits apply.

distractorExplanations.a: Keyboard work involves DSE risks, not WBV.
distractorExplanations.b: Keyboard work involves DSE risks, not WBV.
distractorExplanations.c: Desk reading is not a WBV exposure.

prompt: A night-shift nurse reports fatigue-related errors. Which control best addresses the underlying health risk?

options.text: Review shift length, rest between shifts, workload, and fatigue policy with worker involvement
Add caffeine dispensers only without schedule review
Ignore fatigue because healthcare is always stressful
Mandate overtime to cover vacancies without recovery time

explanation: Fatigue management addresses scheduling, rest, workload, and culture — critical for psychological and physical safety in shift work.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.c: Stress does not remove duty to manage fatigue.
distractorExplanations.d: Excess overtime increases fatigue and error risk.

prompt: Which symptom cluster should trigger urgent referral under a HAVS health surveillance programme?

options.text: Mild thirst after a hot day
Persistent numbness, loss of grip strength, and finger blanching after tool use
Temporary eye dryness from air conditioning
Occasional sneezing from pollen

explanation: HAVS progression includes vascular and neurological symptoms; surveillance aims to detect early signs and restrict exposure.

distractorExplanations.a: Thirst relates to hydration, not HAVS.
distractorExplanations.c: Dry eyes are a DSE/environment issue.
distractorExplanations.d: Pollen allergy is unrelated to vibration.

prompt: An ergonomic improvement for data-entry staff with forearm pain should prioritise:

options.text: Smaller screens placed below desk level
Removal of all wrist support to toughen workers
Adjustable chairs, neutral wrist posture, ergonomic keyboards or rests, and micro-breaks
Increasing keystroke targets without pause

explanation: Forearm and wrist MSDs are reduced by neutral posture, suitable furniture, and breaks from repetitive activity.

distractorExplanations.d: Higher pace without breaks increases RSI risk.

prompt: What is the most suitable control for a worker exposed to 85 dB(A) noise for six hours daily (above lower action value)?

options.text: No controls until exposure reaches 120 dB(A)
Play louder music to mask machine noise
Remove ear defenders to improve communication permanently
Provide suitable hearing protection, training in use, and plan noise reduction measures

explanation: Between lower and upper action values, employers must provide hearing protection and information/training, and assess further reduction.

distractorExplanations.a: Legal action values are far below 120 dB(A).

prompt: A pregnant worker's risk assessment for manual handling should include:

options.text: Review of load limits, posture, rest, medical advice, and task modification as pregnancy progresses
Automatic dismissal from all employment
No changes because pregnancy is unrelated to work
Doubling lifting targets to maintain fitness

explanation: Individual factors require reassessment for pregnant workers — manual handling limits and suitable adjustments are typical.

distractorExplanations.b: Pregnancy is a key individual factor in risk assessment.
distractorExplanations.c: Dismissal on pregnancy grounds is unlawful and unsafe.
distractorExplanations.d: Increased lifting is inappropriate without medical and ergonomic review.

prompt: Poor workplace lighting contributing to eye strain and headaches is best corrected by:

options.text: Removing all windows to eliminate daylight
Balancing ambient and task lighting, reducing glare, and allowing DSE breaks
Maximum brightness on all screens at all times
Discouraging workers from reporting visual discomfort

explanation: Visual health depends on suitable luminance, glare control, and breaks from concentrated screen focus.

distractorExplanations.a: Daylight can be managed with blinds rather than eliminated.
distractorExplanations.c: Maximum brightness can increase glare and fatigue.

prompt: Which indicator best suggests work-related stress rather than only personal issues?

options.text: One worker enjoying a hobby at weekends
Seasonal colds in winter across the site
Multiple workers in the same team reporting high demand, low control, and bullying with rising absence
A single employee's unrelated family celebration

explanation: Clustered symptoms linked to organisational factors (demand, control, relationships, change) indicate psychosocial hazards at work.

distractorExplanations.a: Hobbies do not diagnose organisational stress.
distractorExplanations.b: Hobbies do not diagnose organisational stress.
distractorExplanations.d: Personal events do not explain team-wide trends.

prompt: When delivering manual handling training, the most effective approach is:

options.text: Show a generic video unrelated to site tasks
Test lifting speed only without technique review
Issue a certificate without observation of practice
Combine theory on TILE with practical demonstration using actual workplace loads and tasks

explanation: Task-specific training with practice on real loads improves retention and relevance for NEBOSH and legal competence expectations.

distractorExplanations.a: Generic video alone lacks task specificity.
distractorExplanations.b: Speed testing can encourage unsafe practice.
distractorExplanations.c: Speed testing can encourage unsafe practice.

prompt: A canteen on an industrial site should primarily support worker health by:

options.text: Providing hygienic food preparation areas, seating, hydration, and facilities to eat away from hazardous zones
Locating eating areas inside a chemical storage room
Removing seating to shorten lunch breaks
Banning water to prevent toilet breaks

explanation: Welfare includes safe, hygienic rest and eating areas separated from hazardous processes.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.d: Hydration is essential for health and performance.

prompt: Vibration magnitude from a tool is documented as 8 m/s² A(8) on the datasheet. This value is used to:

options.text: Determine the colour of the tool housing
Calculate exposure time against exposure limit values and plan controls under vibration regulations
Set office DSE break frequencies
Replace the need for maintenance schedules

explanation: Manufacturers' vibration data feeds exposure calculations to compare with ELV/EAV and select controls and surveillance.

distractorExplanations.a: Housing colour is unrelated to vibration dose.
distractorExplanations.c: DSE breaks are separate from HAV assessment.
distractorExplanations.d: Maintenance remains essential for safe tools.

prompt: An employee returning after long-term back injury should:

options.text: Immediately resume maximal lifting without assessment
Be excluded permanently from all duties without review
Receive a return-to-work plan with phased duties, ergonomic review, and medical/OH advice
Receive no workplace adjustments because injury was personal

explanation: Rehabilitation and reasonable adjustments support physical recovery and legal duties for disabled or injured workers.

distractorExplanations.b: Permanent exclusion without assessment may be discriminatory and unnecessary.
distractorExplanations.d: Work-related or aggravated MSDs still need workplace controls.

prompt: Which combination best prevents MSDs in a packaging line?

options.text: Increase conveyor speed and remove all pauses
Provide only a poster stating 'lift safely'
Ignore early reports of wrist discomfort
Workstation height adjustment, job rotation with redesigned low-force tasks, and training on posture

explanation: MSD prevention blends ergonomics, task design, and early intervention on discomfort before chronic injury.

distractorExplanations.a: Higher speed without redesign increases repetition and force.

prompt: Exposure to occupational noise can cause tinnitus. The most appropriate long-term control is:

options.text: Engineering noise reduction at source combined with managed hearing protection programme
Annual bonus for tolerating noise without protection
Relocation of affected workers to louder areas
Ignoring tinnitus because it is not painful

explanation: Tinnitus indicates auditory damage; source reduction and hearing conservation programmes are primary preventive strategies.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.d: Tinnitus is a serious health outcome requiring control.
